nodes.itemHTML: • Analytics without cookies
• Breadcrumbs on Railsstars912 - A simple Ruby on Rails plugin for creating and managing a breadcrumb navigation for a Rails project.
• Gretelstars891 - A Ruby on Rails plugin that makes it easy yet flexible to create breadcrumbs.
• loafstars403 - Manages and displays breadcrumb trails in Rails app - lean & mean.
• Simple Navigationstars874 - A ruby gem for creating navigation (html list, link list or breadcrumbs with multiple levels) for your Rails 2, 3 & 4, Sinatra or Padrino.
• ActiveInteractionstars1.8k - Manage application specific business logic.
• Interactorstars3.1k - Interactor provides a common interface for performing complex interactions in a single request.
• Light Servicestars736 - Series of Actions with an emphasis on simplicity.
• Mutationsstars1.4k - Compose your business logic into commands that sanitize and validate input.
• Surroundedstars213 - Encapsulated related objects in a single system to add behavior during runtime. Extensible implementation of DCI.
• Waterfallstars608 - A slice of functional programming to chain ruby services and blocks, thus providing a new approach to flow control.
• wisperstars3.1k - A micro library providing Ruby objects with Publish-Subscribe capabilities.
• Docilestars396 - A tiny library that lets you map a DSL (domain specific language) to your Ruby objects in a snap.
• dry-rb - dry-rb is a collection of next-generation Ruby libraries, each intended to encapsulate a common task.
• Decent Exposurestars1.8k - A helper for creating declarative interfaces in controllers.
• Respondersstars2k - A set of Rails responders to dry up your application.
• Awesome Ruby @LibHunt - Your go-to Ruby Toolbox. The Awesome Ruby collection + ordering by popularity, additional metadata and comparisons.
• GitHub Trending - Find what repositories the GitHub community is most excited about today.
• Open Source Rails - A gallery of the best open source rack and Ruby on Rails web applications.
• Ruby Bookmarksstars2k - Ruby and Ruby on Rails bookmarks collection.
• RubyDaily - Community driven news.
• RubyFlow - Ruby Programming Community Link Blog.
• Ruby Land - A well maintained Ruby/Rails aggregator that contains news, opinions, tutorials, and more.
• Ruby Rogues - Weekly panel discussion about programming, primarily in Ruby.
• Ruby Weekly - A free, once–weekly e-mail round-up of Ruby news and articles.
• The Ruby Bibliography - Research papers about Ruby.
• The Ruby Toolbox - A comprehensive catalog of Ruby and Rails plug-ins, gems, tools and resources for Ruby developers with popularity ratings based on GitHub watchers and Gem downloads.
• Eventide Project - Pub/sub, event sourcing, and evented autonomous services backed by the Message DBstars1.5k message store.

• Rails Event Store (RES)stars1.2k - A library for publishing, consuming, storing and retrieving events. It's your best companion for going with an event-driven architecture for your Rails application.
• AppSignal - Better monitoring for your Rails applications.
• Codacy - Automated Code Review for Ruby, Rails, JS, PHP, Python etc. Security, Coverage & Quality.
• CodeClimate - Quality & security analysis for Ruby on Rails and Javascript.
• GitHub - Powerful collaboration, code review, and code management for open source and private projects.
• Gitlab CI - Integrate with your GitLab to run tests for your projects.
• GitLab - Open source software to collaborate on code.
• HoundCI - Review your Ruby code for style guide violations.
• Inch CI - Documentation badges for Ruby projects.
• OctoLinkerstars5.1k - Navigate through projects on GitHub.com efficiently with the OctoLinker browser extension.
• SemaphoreCI - Hosted continuous integration and deployment service for open source and private projects.
• Sider - Automated Code Review with GitHub PR. Monitoring Style Violations, Quality, Security, Dependencies.
• Travis CI - Test and Deploy Your Code with Confidence.
• Cellsstars3k - View Components for Rails.
• Komponentstars426 - An opinionated way of organizing front-end code in Rails, based on components.
• ViewComponentstars2.7k - View components for Rails.
• active_link_tostars825 - View helper to manage "active" state of a link.
• Trailblazerstars3.3k - Trailblazer is a thin layer on top of Rails. It gently enforces encapsulation, an intuitive code structure and gives you an object-oriented architecture.
• AnyCable – Polyglot replacement for Ruby WebSocket servers with Action Cable protocol.
• ruby-netsnmpstars32 - SNMP library in ruby (v1, v2c, v3).

• snmpclitoolsstars37 - This is a collection of command-line SNMP tools written in pure-Python. The tools mimic their famous Net-SNMP counterparts. It includes snmpget.py, snmpset.py, snmpwalk.py, snmpbulkwalk.py, snmptrap.py, and snmptranslate.py, see here for more details.
• snmp-parserstars37 - SNMP parser written in rust with nom parser combinator framework.
• sleepinggenius2/gosmi)stars63 - MIB parser in Go language.
• posteo/go-agentxstars43 - A library with a pure Go implementation of the AgentX-Protocol.
• trapperkeeperstars79 - A suite of tools for ingesting and displaying SNMP traps. This is designed as a replacement for snmptrapd and to supplement existing stateful monitoring solutions.
• snmpsimstars307 - This is a pure-Python, open source and free implementation of SNMP agents simulator distributed under 2-clause BSD license.

• snmpfwdstars62 - The SNMP Proxy Forwarder tool works as an application-level proxy with a built-in SNMP message router. Typical use case for an SNMP proxy is to work as an application-level firewall or a protocol translator that enables SNMPv3 access to a SNMPv1/SNMPv2c entity or vice versa.
• snmpwnstars200 - SNMPwn is an SNMPv3 user enumerator and attack tool.

• ireasoning/MIB Browser - This tool allows users to load standard, proprietary MIBs, and even some mal-formed MIBs. It also allows them to issue SNMP requests to retrieve agent's data, or make changes to the agent. Free for personal use.
• pysnmpstars489 - This is a pure-Python, open source and free implementation of v1/v2c/v3 SNMP engine distributed under 2-clause BSD license.
• pysmistars110 - PySMI is a pure-Python implementation of SNMP SMI MIB parser.
• net-snmp Python bindings - Net-SNMP 5.4 and later contains Python bindings in the "python" sub-directory, though, they are not built by default.
• puresnmpstars70 - Pure Python3 SNMPv2 library without any dependencies.
• snimpystars170 - Snimpy is a Python-based tool providing a simple interface to build SNMP query.
• python-netsnmpagentstars62 - This package allows to write net-snmp subagents in Python.
• hnmpstars45 - HNMP is a high-level Python library to ease the pain of retrieving and processing data from SNMP-capable devices such as network switches, routers, and printers.
• T3X - A small, portable, procedural, block-structured, recursive, almost typeless, and to some degree object-oriented programming language by Nils M. Holm.
• tmux-modalstars90 - Execute complex tmux commands in just a few keystrokes with a modal mode that is designed to be efficient, easy to remember and comfortable.
• Aim - Aim is an open source experiment tracking tool.
• swiperstars32.9k - modern mobile touch slider
• swiperstars32.9k - Official Swiper component for Vue 3. Tree shakable, SSR support, typing, a11y and a lot more
• gpanders/editorconfig.nvimstars210 - An EditorConfig plugin for Neovim written in Fennel.
• AssemblyScript - Rules for enforcing restrictions on TypeScript toward AssemblyScript compliance.
• eslint-interactivestars112 - The CLI tool to fix huge number of ESLint errors.
• Uni Paderborn. Quantum Complexity Theory. Winter 2020 - CS Masters level lectures on topics including Boson sampling, quantum interactive proofs, and quantum merlin arthur.
• Henry Yuen. The Complexity of Entanglement. Fall 2020 - Focuses on cutting edge topics in quantum information that relate to Complexity of Entanglement. - see this class also
• PGMaxstars58 - A framework for building discrete Probabilistic Graphical Models (PGM's) and running inference inference on them via JAX.
• Probabilistic Sufficient Explanations - IJCAI'21, 2021. [All Versions].
